docs.title: f5xc-auth
docs.text: Shared authentication library for F5 Distributed Cloud MCP servers. XDG-compliant profile management and credential handling.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth provides a unified authentication layer for F5 Distributed Cloud (F5 XC) applications. It handles credential management, profile storage, and HTTP client configuration following XDG Base Directory specifications.

docs.title: XDG-Compliant Storage
docs.text: Profiles stored in ~/.config/f5xc/profiles/ following XDG Base Directory specification for cross-platform compatibility.

docs.title: Multiple Auth Methods
docs.text: Support for API tokens, P12 certificate bundles, and certificate/key pairs for flexible authentication options.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Override profile settings with environment variables for CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments.

docs.title: URL Normalization
docs.text: Automatically handles various F5 XC tenant URL formats, normalizing them for consistent API access.

docs.title: TLS Configuration
docs.text: Custom CA bundles for enterprise environments and insecure mode for staging/development.

docs.title: HTTP Client
docs.text: Pre-configured Axios HTTP client with automatic authentication header injection and retry logic.

docs.title: Quick Install
docs.text: npm install @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth

docs.title: Basic Usage
docs.text:
import { CredentialManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();
await credentialManager.initialize();

if (credentialManager.isAuthenticated()) {
  console.log(`Authenticated as: ${credentialManager.getTenant()}`);
  console.log(`API URL: ${credentialManager.getApiUrl()}`);
}

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Node.js: 18 or later
  • F5 Distributed Cloud: Valid tenant credentials

docs.title: Getting Help
docs.text:
• Getting Started Guide - Installation and initial setup
  • API Reference - Complete API documentation
  • GitHub Issues - Report bugs or request features

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: Complete API documentation for @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The library exports three main components:

Component Description CredentialManager Manages authentication state and credential resolution ProfileManager Handles profile CRUD operations and storage HttpClient Pre-configured Axios client with auth headers

docs.title: Imports
docs.text:
// Main exports
import {
  CredentialManager,
  createHttpClient,
  getProfileManager
} from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

// Types
import type {
  Profile,
  ProfileConfig,
  ProfileResult,
  Credentials,
  AuthMode,
  HttpClientConfig,
  ApiResponse
} from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

// Utility functions
import {
  normalizeApiUrl,
  normalizeTenantUrl,
  extractTenantFromUrl
} from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

docs.title: Authentication Modes
docs.text:
enum AuthMode {
  NONE = "none",           // Documentation mode
  TOKEN = "token",         // API token authentication
  CERTIFICATE = "certificate"  // mTLS authentication
}

docs.title: Module Structure
docs.text:
@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth
├── CredentialManager     # Auth state management
│   ├── initialize()
│   ├── isAuthenticated()
│   ├── getAuthMode()
│   ├── getApiUrl()
│   ├── getTenant()
│   └── ...
├── ProfileManager        # Profile storage
│   ├── list()
│   ├── get()
│   ├── save()
│   ├── delete()
│   ├── setActive()
│   └── ...
├── HttpClient           # API requests
│   ├── get()
│   ├── post()
│   ├── put()
│   ├── delete()
│   └── ...
└── Utilities
    ├── normalizeApiUrl()
    ├── normalizeTenantUrl()
    └── extractTenantFromUrl()

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Variable Type Description F5XC_API_URL string F5 XC tenant URL F5XC_API_TOKEN string API token F5XC_P12_BUNDLE string Path to P12 certificate F5XC_CERT string Path to certificate file F5XC_KEY string Path to private key file F5XC_NAMESPACE string Default namespace F5XC_TLS_INSECURE boolean Disable TLS verification F5XC_CA_BUNDLE string Path to custom CA bundle

docs.title: Error Types
docs.text:
// API request errors
class F5XCApiError extends Error {
  status?: number;
  data?: unknown;
}

// Authentication errors
class AuthenticationError extends Error {}

// SSL/TLS errors (wrapped with guidance)
class SSLError extends Error {}

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• CredentialManager - Full credential management API
  • ProfileManager - Profile storage and switching
  • HttpClient - Making authenticated API requests

docs.title: CredentialManager
docs.text: The CredentialManager class handles authentication credential resolution and state management.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: import { CredentialManager, AuthMode } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();

Creates a new CredentialManager instance. Credentials are not loaded until initialize() is called.

docs.title: initialize()
docs.text:
async initialize(): Promise<void>

Loads credentials from environment variables or active profile. Must be called before accessing credentials.

Example:

const cm = new CredentialManager();
await cm.initialize();

docs.title: isAuthenticated()
docs.text:
isAuthenticated(): boolean

Returns true if valid credentials are loaded.

Returns: boolean

Example:

if (cm.isAuthenticated()) {
  // Make API calls
}

docs.title: getAuthMode()
docs.text:
getAuthMode(): AuthMode

Returns the current authentication mode.

Returns: AuthMode - One of:

  • AuthMode.NONE - No authentication (documentation mode)
  • AuthMode.TOKEN - API token authentication
  • AuthMode.CERTIFICATE - Certificate/mTLS authentication

Example:

const mode = cm.getAuthMode();
if (mode === AuthMode.TOKEN) {
  console.log('Using API token authentication');
}

docs.title: getApiUrl()
docs.text:
getApiUrl(): string | null

Returns the normalized API URL with /api suffix.

Returns: string | null

Example:

const apiUrl = cm.getApiUrl();
// Returns: "https://tenant.console.ves.volterra.io/api"

docs.title: getTenant()
docs.text:
getTenant(): string | null

Extracts the tenant name from the API URL.

Returns: string | null

Example:

const tenant = cm.getTenant();
// Returns: "tenant"

docs.title: getNamespace()
docs.text:
getNamespace(): string | null

Returns the default namespace.

Returns: string | null

docs.title: getToken()
docs.text:
getToken(): string | null

Returns the API token (for token authentication).

Returns: string | null

Security

Do not log or expose the token in application output.

docs.title: getP12Certificate()
docs.text:
getP12Certificate(): Buffer | null

Returns the P12 certificate buffer (for certificate authentication).

Returns: Buffer | null

docs.title: getCert() / getKey()
docs.text:
getCert(): string | null
getKey(): string | null

Returns certificate and private key content (for mTLS with separate files).

Returns: string | null

docs.title: getTlsInsecure()
docs.text:
getTlsInsecure(): boolean

Returns whether TLS certificate verification is disabled.

Returns: boolean

Warning

Only use TLS insecure mode for staging/development environments.

docs.title: getCaBundle()
docs.text:
getCaBundle(): Buffer | null

Returns the custom CA bundle for TLS verification.

Returns: Buffer | null

docs.title: getActiveProfile()
docs.text:
getActiveProfile(): string | null

Returns the name of the active profile, or null if credentials are from environment variables.

Returns: string | null

docs.title: getCredentials()
docs.text:
getCredentials(): Readonly<Credentials>

Returns a read-only copy of all credentials.

Returns: Readonly<Credentials>

docs.title: reload()
docs.text:
async reload(): Promise<void>

Reloads credentials from environment/profile. Useful when credentials change during runtime.

Example:

// After environment changes
await cm.reload();

docs.title: Credential Priority
docs.text:
Credentials are resolved in this order:

  1. Environment Variables (highest priority)
  2. Active Profile from ~/.config/f5xc/
  3. Documentation Mode (no credentials - lowest)

docs.title: AuthMode
docs.text:
enum AuthMode {
  NONE = "none",
  TOKEN = "token",
  CERTIFICATE = "certificate"
}

docs.title: Credentials
docs.text:
interface Credentials {
  mode: AuthMode;
  apiUrl: string | null;
  token: string | null;
  p12Certificate: Buffer | null;
  cert: string | null;
  key: string | null;
  namespace: string | null;
  tlsInsecure: boolean;
  caBundle: Buffer | null;
}

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import { CredentialManager, AuthMode } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function checkAuth() {
  const cm = new CredentialManager();
  await cm.initialize();

  console.log('Authentication Status:');
  console.log(`  Mode: ${cm.getAuthMode()}`);
  console.log(`  Authenticated: ${cm.isAuthenticated()}`);

  if (cm.isAuthenticated()) {
    console.log(`  Tenant: ${cm.getTenant()}`);
    console.log(`  API URL: ${cm.getApiUrl()}`);
    console.log(`  Namespace: ${cm.getNamespace() || 'default'}`);

    const profile = cm.getActiveProfile();
    if (profile) {
      console.log(`  Profile: ${profile}`);
    } else {
      console.log('  Source: Environment variables');
    }
  }
}

checkAuth();

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• ProfileManager - Managing stored profiles
  • HttpClient - Making authenticated requests

docs.title: HttpClient
docs.text: The HttpClient class provides a pre-configured Axios instance for making authenticated API requests to F5 Distributed Cloud.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: import { createHttpClient, CredentialManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

docs.title: Creating an Instance
docs.text:
const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();
await credentialManager.initialize();

const httpClient = createHttpClient(credentialManager, {
  timeout: 30000,
  debug: true
});

docs.title: Constructor Options
docs.text:
interface HttpClientConfig {
  timeout?: number;          // Request timeout in ms (default: 30000)
  headers?: Record<string, string>;  // Custom headers
  debug?: boolean;           // Enable request/response logging
}
Option Type Default Description timeout number 30000 Request timeout in milliseconds headers object {} Custom headers for all requests debug boolean false Enable debug logging

docs.title: isAvailable()
docs.text:
isAvailable(): boolean

Returns true if the client has valid credentials and can make requests.

Example:

if (!httpClient.isAvailable()) {
  console.log('Running in documentation mode - API calls disabled');
}

docs.title: get()
docs.text:
GET

async get<T>(path: string, config?: AxiosRequestConfig): Promise<ApiResponse<T>>

Makes a GET request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description path string API endpoint path config AxiosRequestConfig Optional Axios config

Example:

const response = await httpClient.get('/web/namespaces');
console.log(response.data);

docs.title: post()
docs.text:
POST

async post<T>(path: string, data?: unknown, config?: AxiosRequestConfig): Promise<ApiResponse<T>>

Makes a POST request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description path string API endpoint path data unknown Request body config AxiosRequestConfig Optional Axios config

Example:

const response = await httpClient.post('/config/namespaces', {
  metadata: { name: 'my-namespace' },
  spec: {}
});

docs.title: put()
docs.text:
PUT

async put<T>(path: string, data?: unknown, config?: AxiosRequestConfig): Promise<ApiResponse<T>>

Makes a PUT request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description path string API endpoint path data unknown Request body config AxiosRequestConfig Optional Axios config

docs.title: delete()
docs.text:
DELETE

async delete<T>(path: string, config?: AxiosRequestConfig): Promise<ApiResponse<T>>

Makes a DELETE request.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description path string API endpoint path config AxiosRequestConfig Optional Axios config

docs.title: getAxiosInstance()
docs.text:
getAxiosInstance(): AxiosInstance | null

Returns the underlying Axios instance for advanced usage.

Example:

const axios = httpClient.getAxiosInstance();
if (axios) {
  // Use Axios directly
  axios.interceptors.request.use(config => {
    console.log('Request:', config.url);
    return config;
  });
}

docs.title: Response Format
docs.text:
All request methods return an ApiResponse object:

interface ApiResponse<T> {
  data: T;                    // Response data
  status: number;             // HTTP status code
  headers: Record<string, string>;  // Response headers
  duration: number;           // Request duration in ms
}

Example:

const response = await httpClient.get('/web/namespaces');
console.log(`Status: ${response.status}`);
console.log(`Duration: ${response.duration}ms`);
console.log(`Data:`, response.data);

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
The client throws typed errors for different failure scenarios:

import { F5XCApiError, AuthenticationError } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

try {
  const response = await httpClient.get('/web/namespaces');
} catch (error) {
  if (error instanceof AuthenticationError) {
    console.error('Authentication failed:', error.message);
  } else if (error instanceof F5XCApiError) {
    console.error(`API error (${error.status}):`, error.message);
    console.error('Response data:', error.data);
  } else {
    console.error('Unknown error:', error);
  }
}

docs.title: AuthenticationError
docs.text:
Thrown when credentials are missing or invalid:

class AuthenticationError extends Error {
  constructor(message: string);
}

docs.title: F5XCApiError
docs.text:
Thrown for API response errors:

class F5XCApiError extends Error {
  status?: number;
  data?: unknown;
  constructor(message: string, status?: number, data?: unknown);
}

docs.title: TLS Configuration
docs.text: The client automatically configures TLS based on credentials:

docs.title: Custom CA Bundle
docs.text:
// Set via environment
export F5XC_CA_BUNDLE=/path/to/ca-bundle.crt

// Or in profile
{
  "name": "enterprise",
  "apiUrl": "https://tenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "...",
  "caBundle": "/path/to/ca-bundle.crt"
}

docs.title: Insecure Mode
docs.text:
Warning

Only use for staging/development environments!

// Set via environment
export F5XC_TLS_INSECURE=true

// Or in profile
{
  "name": "staging",
  "apiUrl": "https://staging.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "...",
  "tlsInsecure": true
}

docs.title: Authentication Modes
docs.text: The client automatically configures authentication based on credentials:

docs.title: Token Authentication
docs.text:
// Headers set automatically:
// Authorization: APIToken <token>

docs.title: Certificate Authentication (mTLS)
docs.text:
// HTTPS agent configured with:
// - pfx: P12 certificate buffer, or
// - cert/key: Certificate and private key

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import {
  CredentialManager,
  createHttpClient,
  F5XCApiError,
  AuthenticationError
} from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function listNamespaces() {
  // Initialize credentials
  const cm = new CredentialManager();
  await cm.initialize();

  // Create HTTP client
  const client = createHttpClient(cm, {
    timeout: 60000,
    debug: process.env.DEBUG === 'true'
  });

  // Check availability
  if (!client.isAvailable()) {
    console.log('No credentials configured');
    console.log('Set F5XC_API_URL and F5XC_API_TOKEN to enable API calls');
    return;
  }

  try {
    // Make request
    const response = await client.get<{ items: Array<{ name: string }> }>(
      '/web/namespaces'
    );

    console.log(`Found ${response.data.items?.length || 0} namespaces`);
    console.log(`Request took ${response.duration}ms`);

    response.data.items?.forEach(ns => {
      console.log(`  - ${ns.name}`);
    });

  } catch (error) {
    if (error instanceof AuthenticationError) {
      console.error('Auth error:', error.message);
    } else if (error instanceof F5XCApiError) {
      console.error(`API error ${error.status}:`, error.message);
    } else {
      throw error;
    }
  }
}

listNamespaces();

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• CredentialManager - Credential management
  • Configuration - HTTP client options

docs.title: ProfileManager
docs.text: The ProfileManager class handles profile CRUD operations with secure XDG-compliant file storage.

docs.title: Import
docs.text: import { getProfileManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

docs.title: Getting the Instance
docs.text:
The ProfileManager uses a singleton pattern. Use getProfileManager() to get the shared instance:

const profileManager = getProfileManager();

docs.title: list()
docs.text:
async list(): Promise<Profile[]>

Lists all saved profiles, sorted alphabetically by name.

Returns: Profile[]

Example:

const profiles = await profileManager.list();
profiles.forEach(p => console.log(p.name));

docs.title: get()
docs.text:
async get(name: string): Promise<Profile | null>

Gets a profile by name. Supports both JSON and YAML profile files.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description name string Profile name

Returns: Profile | null

Example:

const profile = await profileManager.get('production');
if (profile) {
  console.log(profile.apiUrl);
}

docs.title: save()
docs.text:
async save(profile: Profile): Promise<ProfileResult>

Saves a new profile or updates an existing one.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description profile Profile Profile data to save

Returns: ProfileResult

Validation Rules:

  • Name: alphanumeric, dashes, underscores only (max 64 chars)
  • API URL: Must be valid HTTP/HTTPS URL
  • Authentication: At least one auth method required

Example:

const result = await profileManager.save({
  name: 'production',
  apiUrl: 'https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: 'my-api-token',
  defaultNamespace: 'my-namespace'
});

if (result.success) {
  console.log(result.message);
} else {
  console.error('Failed:', result.message);
}

docs.title: delete()
docs.text:
async delete(name: string): Promise<ProfileResult>

Deletes a profile by name.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description name string Profile name to delete

Returns: ProfileResult

Note

Cannot delete the currently active profile. Switch to another profile first.

Example:

const result = await profileManager.delete('old-profile');
if (!result.success) {
  console.error(result.message);
}

docs.title: setActive()
docs.text:
async setActive(name: string): Promise<ProfileResult>

Sets a profile as the active profile.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description name string Profile name to activate

Returns: ProfileResult

Example:

const result = await profileManager.setActive('production');
if (result.success) {
  console.log('Switched to production profile');
}

docs.title: getActive()
docs.text:
async getActive(): Promise<string | null>

Gets the name of the currently active profile.

Returns: string | null

Example:

const activeName = await profileManager.getActive();
console.log(`Active profile: ${activeName || 'none'}`);

docs.title: getActiveProfile()
docs.text:
async getActiveProfile(): Promise<Profile | null>

Gets the full profile data for the active profile.

Returns: Profile | null

Example:

const profile = await profileManager.getActiveProfile();
if (profile) {
  console.log(`Connected to: ${profile.apiUrl}`);
}

docs.title: exists()
docs.text:
async exists(name: string): Promise<boolean>

Checks if a profile exists.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description name string Profile name to check

Returns: boolean

docs.title: maskProfile()
docs.text:
maskProfile(profile: Profile): Record<string, string>

Returns a profile with sensitive fields masked for display.

Parameters:

Parameter Type Description profile Profile Profile to mask

Returns: Record<string, string>

Example:

const profile = await profileManager.get('production');
if (profile) {
  const masked = profileManager.maskProfile(profile);
  console.log(masked);
  // { name: 'production', apiUrl: '...', apiToken: '****abcd' }
}

docs.title: Profile
docs.text:
interface Profile {
  name: string;
  apiUrl: string;
  apiToken?: string;
  p12Bundle?: string;
  cert?: string;
  key?: string;
  defaultNamespace?: string;
  tlsInsecure?: boolean;
  caBundle?: string;
}

docs.title: ProfileResult
docs.text:
interface ProfileResult {
  success: boolean;
  message: string;
  profile?: Profile;
  profiles?: Profile[];
}

docs.title: ProfileConfig
docs.text:
interface ProfileConfig {
  configDir: string;
  profilesDir: string;
  activeProfileFile: string;
}

docs.title: Storage Location
docs.text:
Profiles are stored in XDG-compliant directories:

Platform Location Linux/macOS ~/.config/f5xc/profiles/ Windows %APPDATA%\f5xc\profiles\

docs.title: File Format
docs.text:
Profiles are stored as JSON files with 0600 permissions:

{
  "name": "production",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "your-api-token",
  "defaultNamespace": "my-namespace"
}

YAML format is also supported for reading:

name: production
api_url: https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io
api_token: your-api-token
default_namespace: my-namespace

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
import { getProfileManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function manageProfiles() {
  const pm = getProfileManager();

  // List existing profiles
  const profiles = await pm.list();
  console.log('Existing profiles:', profiles.map(p => p.name));

  // Create a new profile
  const saveResult = await pm.save({
    name: 'staging',
    apiUrl: 'https://staging.console.ves.volterra.io',
    apiToken: process.env.STAGING_TOKEN!,
    defaultNamespace: 'test'
  });

  if (!saveResult.success) {
    console.error('Failed to save:', saveResult.message);
    return;
  }

  // Set as active
  await pm.setActive('staging');

  // Get current active profile
  const active = await pm.getActiveProfile();
  if (active) {
    const masked = pm.maskProfile(active);
    console.log('Active profile:', masked);
  }
}

manageProfiles();

docs.title: Related
docs.text:
• CredentialManager - Using credentials
  • Configuration Guide - Profile format details

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: This guide will help you install and configure @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth for your F5 Distributed Cloud projects.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
Before you begin, ensure you have:

  • Node.js 18 or later installed
  • npm or yarn package manager
  • F5 Distributed Cloud tenant access with valid credentials

docs.title: What You'll Learn
docs.text:
1. Installation - How to install the package
  2. Quick Start - Basic usage and first steps
  3. Configuration - Profile setup and environment variables

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
The f5xc-auth library provides three main components:

Component Purpose CredentialManager Manages authentication state and credential resolution ProfileManager Handles profile CRUD operations and storage HttpClient Pre-configured Axios client with auth headers

docs.title: Credential Priority
docs.text:
The library resolves credentials in the following order:

  1. Environment Variables (highest priority)
  2. Active Profile from ~/.config/f5xc/
  3. Documentation Mode (no credentials - lowest priority)

This allows you to override profile settings in CI/CD pipelines while maintaining local development profiles.

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Start with the Installation Guide to add the package to your project.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: This guide covers all configuration options for f5xc-auth.

docs.title: Profile Location
docs.text:
Profiles are stored in XDG-compliant directories:

Platform Location Linux ~/.config/f5xc/profiles/ macOS ~/.config/f5xc/profiles/ Windows %APPDATA%\f5xc\profiles\

docs.title: Profile Schema
docs.text:
A profile is a JSON file with the following structure:

{
  "name": "production",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "your-api-token",
  "defaultNamespace": "my-namespace",
  "tlsInsecure": false,
  "caBundle": "/path/to/ca-bundle.crt"
}

docs.title: Required Fields
docs.text: Field Type Description name string Unique profile identifier apiUrl string F5 XC tenant URL

docs.title: Authentication Fields
docs.text:
You must provide one of the following authentication methods:

API TokenP12 CertificateCert + Key 
{
  "name": "token-auth",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "your-api-token"
}

{
  "name": "p12-auth",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "p12Bundle": "/path/to/certificate.p12"
}

{
  "name": "cert-auth",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "cert": "/path/to/certificate.pem",
  "key": "/path/to/private-key.pem"
}

docs.title: Optional Fields
docs.text: Field Type Default Description defaultNamespace string - Default namespace for API operations tlsInsecure boolean false Disable TLS verification (staging only) caBundle string - Path to custom CA bundle

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Environment variables override profile settings:

Variable Description Example F5XC_API_URL Tenant URL https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io F5XC_API_TOKEN API token your-api-token F5XC_P12_BUNDLE P12 certificate path /path/to/cert.p12 F5XC_CERT Certificate file path /path/to/cert.pem F5XC_KEY Private key file path /path/to/key.pem F5XC_NAMESPACE Default namespace my-namespace F5XC_TLS_INSECURE Disable TLS verification true F5XC_CA_BUNDLE Custom CA bundle path /path/to/ca-bundle.crt

docs.title: Priority Order
docs.text:
1. Environment Variables (highest)
  2. Active Profile
  3. Documentation Mode (no credentials)

docs.title: URL Normalization
docs.text:
The library automatically normalizes various URL formats:

Input Normalized Output mytenant https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io/ https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io

docs.title: File Permissions
docs.text:
Profile files are created with secure permissions:

  • Profile files: 0600 (owner read/write only)
  • Config directory: 0700 (owner access only)

docs.title: Token Masking
docs.text:
When displaying credentials, tokens are masked to show only the last 4 characters:

Token: ****-****-****-abcd

docs.title: TLS Configuration
docs.text:
Insecure Mode

Only enable tlsInsecure for staging/development environments. Never use in production.

For enterprise environments with custom CA certificates:

{
  "name": "enterprise",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "your-token",
  "caBundle": "/etc/pki/tls/certs/enterprise-ca.crt"
}

docs.title: HTTP Client Configuration
docs.text:
The HTTP client accepts additional options:

import { createHttpClient } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const httpClient = createHttpClient(credentialManager, {
  timeout: 30000,      // Request timeout in ms
  debug: true,         // Enable debug logging
  retries: 3,          // Number of retry attempts
  retryDelay: 1000     // Delay between retries in ms
});

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Authentication Methods - Detailed auth method guide
  • Profile Management - Advanced profile operations
  • API Reference - Complete API documentation

docs.title: Package Manager
docs.text:
npmyarnpnpm 
npm install @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth

yarn add @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth

pnpm add @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Version Node.js >= 18.0.0 TypeScript >= 5.0 (optional)

docs.title: Dependencies
docs.text:
The library has minimal dependencies:

  • axios - HTTP client for API requests
  • yaml - YAML parsing for configuration files

docs.title: TypeScript Support
docs.text:
Full TypeScript support is included out of the box. Type definitions are bundled with the package.

import type { Profile, ProfileConfig } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

docs.title: ESM Module
docs.text:
This package is distributed as an ES Module. Ensure your project is configured for ESM:

package.json
{
  "type": "module"
}

Or use the .mjs extension for your files.

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
After installation, verify the package is working:

import { CredentialManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const cm = new CredentialManager();
console.log('f5xc-auth installed successfully!');

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Continue to the Quick Start Guide to learn basic usage.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide walks you through basic usage of f5xc-auth to authenticate with F5 Distributed Cloud.

docs.title: Basic Authentication
docs.text:
The simplest way to authenticate is using the CredentialManager:

import { CredentialManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function main() {
  const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();
  await credentialManager.initialize();

  if (credentialManager.isAuthenticated()) {
    console.log(`Tenant: ${credentialManager.getTenant()}`);
    console.log(`API URL: ${credentialManager.getApiUrl()}`);
    console.log(`Namespace: ${credentialManager.getNamespace()}`);
  } else {
    console.log('Not authenticated');
  }
}

main();

docs.title: Create Your First Profile
docs.text:
Profiles store your F5 XC credentials. Create one using the ProfileManager:

import { getProfileManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function createProfile() {
  const profileManager = getProfileManager();

  await profileManager.save({
    name: 'my-tenant',
    apiUrl: 'https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io',
    apiToken: 'your-api-token-here',
    defaultNamespace: 'default'
  });

  // Set as active profile
  await profileManager.setActive('my-tenant');

  console.log('Profile created and activated!');
}

createProfile();

docs.title: Make API Requests
docs.text:
Use the HTTP client to make authenticated requests:

import { CredentialManager, createHttpClient } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function fetchNamespaces() {
  const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();
  await credentialManager.initialize();

  const httpClient = createHttpClient(credentialManager);

  if (httpClient.isAvailable()) {
    const response = await httpClient.get('/web/namespaces');
    console.log('Namespaces:', response.data);
  }
}

fetchNamespaces();

docs.title: Using Environment Variables
docs.text:
You can authenticate using environment variables instead of profiles:

export F5XC_API_URL="https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
export F5XC_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"
export F5XC_NAMESPACE="default"

Then in your code:

import { CredentialManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();
await credentialManager.initialize();

// Credentials are automatically loaded from environment
console.log(credentialManager.isAuthenticated()); // true

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
Here's a complete example that checks authentication and makes a request:

import {
  CredentialManager,
  createHttpClient,
  getProfileManager
} from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function main() {
  // Initialize credential manager
  const credentialManager = new CredentialManager();
  await credentialManager.initialize();

  // Check authentication status
  if (!credentialManager.isAuthenticated()) {
    console.log('No credentials found. Creating profile...');

    const profileManager = getProfileManager();
    await profileManager.save({
      name: 'demo',
      apiUrl: process.env.F5XC_API_URL || 'https://demo.console.ves.volterra.io',
      apiToken: process.env.F5XC_API_TOKEN || 'your-token',
      defaultNamespace: 'default'
    });
    await profileManager.setActive('demo');

    // Re-initialize to pick up new profile
    await credentialManager.initialize();
  }

  // Create HTTP client
  const httpClient = createHttpClient(credentialManager);

  if (httpClient.isAvailable()) {
    try {
      // Fetch namespaces
      const response = await httpClient.get('/web/namespaces');
      console.log('Available namespaces:', response.data.items?.length || 0);
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('API request failed:', error.message);
    }
  }
}

main();

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Configuration - Learn about all configuration options
  • Authentication Methods - Explore different auth methods
  • API Reference - Full API documentation

docs.title: Guides
docs.text: Practical guides for using @robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth in different scenarios.

docs.title: Authentication Methods
docs.text: Learn about the different ways to authenticate with F5 Distributed Cloud: API tokens, P12 certificates, and certificate/key pairs.

docs.title: Profile Management
docs.text: Manage multiple F5 XC profiles for different tenants and environments. Learn to switch between profiles seamlessly.

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: Configure authentication through environment variables for CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments.

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text: Security considerations for credential storage, TLS configuration, and production deployments.

docs.title: Local Development
docs.text:
For local development, create a profile with your F5 XC credentials:

import { getProfileManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const pm = getProfileManager();
await pm.save({
  name: 'dev',
  apiUrl: 'https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: 'your-token'
});
await pm.setActive('dev');

docs.title: CI/CD Pipelines
docs.text:
Use environment variables in CI/CD pipelines:

# GitHub Actions example
env:
  F5XC_API_URL: ${{ secrets.F5XC_API_URL }}
  F5XC_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.F5XC_API_TOKEN }}

docs.title: Multiple Tenants
docs.text:
Switch between different F5 XC tenants:

const pm = getProfileManager();

// List available profiles
const profiles = await pm.list();

// Switch to a different tenant
await pm.setActive('production');

docs.title: Enterprise Environments
docs.text:
For enterprise environments with custom CA certificates:

await pm.save({
  name: 'enterprise',
  apiUrl: 'https://internal.tenant.com',
  apiToken: 'your-token',
  caBundle: '/etc/pki/tls/certs/enterprise-ca.crt'
});

docs.title: Authentication Methods
docs.text: This guide covers the different authentication methods supported by f5xc-auth.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
F5 Distributed Cloud supports three authentication methods:

Method Use Case Security Level API Token Most common, easy to manage High P12 Certificate Legacy, certificate-based Very High Cert + Key mTLS with separate files Very High

docs.title: API Token Authentication
docs.text: API tokens are the most common and recommended authentication method.

docs.title: Getting an API Token
docs.text:
1. Log in to your F5 XC Console
  2. Navigate to Administration > Personal Management > Credentials
  3. Click Add Credentials
  4. Select API Token
  5. Set an expiration date and click Generate
  6. Copy the token immediately (it won't be shown again)

docs.title: Using API Tokens
Token Best Practices
docs.text:
• Set appropriate expiration dates
  • Rotate tokens regularly
  • Use different tokens for different environments
  • Never commit tokens to version control

docs.title: P12 Certificate Authentication
docs.text: P12 (PKCS#12) certificates provide certificate-based mTLS authentication.

docs.title: Getting a P12 Certificate
docs.text:
1. Log in to your F5 XC Console
  2. Navigate to Administration > Personal Management > Credentials
  3. Click Add Credentials
  4. Select API Certificate
  5. Download the P12 file and note the password (if any)

docs.title: Using P12 Certificates
docs.text:
ProfileEnvironment Variable 
{
  "name": "cert-auth",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "p12Bundle": "/path/to/certificate.p12"
}

export F5XC_API_URL="https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
export F5XC_P12_BUNDLE="/path/to/certificate.p12"

Password Support

F5 XC P12 certificates typically don't require a password. If your P12 file has a password, you may need to extract the cert/key separately.

docs.title: Certificate + Key Authentication
docs.text: For mTLS with separate certificate and private key files.

docs.title: Extracting from P12
docs.text:
If you have a P12 file, you can extract the certificate and key:

# Extract certificate
openssl pkcs12 -in certificate.p12 -clcerts -nokeys -out cert.pem

# Extract private key
openssl pkcs12 -in certificate.p12 -nocerts -nodes -out key.pem

docs.title: Using Cert + Key
docs.text:
ProfileEnvironment Variable 
{
  "name": "mtls-auth",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "cert": "/path/to/cert.pem",
  "key": "/path/to/key.pem"
}

export F5XC_API_URL="https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
export F5XC_CERT="/path/to/cert.pem"
export F5XC_KEY="/path/to/key.pem"

docs.title: Authentication Priority
docs.text:
When multiple authentication methods are configured, they're used in this order:

  1. API Token (if both token and certificate are provided)
  2. P12 Certificate
  3. Cert + Key pair

docs.title: Checking Authentication Status
docs.text:
import { CredentialManager, AuthMode } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const cm = new CredentialManager();
await cm.initialize();

console.log('Authenticated:', cm.isAuthenticated());
console.log('Auth Mode:', cm.getAuthMode());

switch (cm.getAuthMode()) {
  case AuthMode.TOKEN:
    console.log('Using API token authentication');
    break;
  case AuthMode.CERTIFICATE:
    console.log('Using certificate authentication');
    break;
  case AuthMode.NONE:
    console.log('No authentication (documentation mode)');
    break;
}

docs.title: Fallback Behavior
docs.text:
The library supports automatic fallback when a certificate fails to load:

// If P12 certificate fails to load but apiToken is also provided,
// the library will fall back to token authentication
{
  "name": "fallback-profile",
  "apiUrl": "https://tenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "p12Bundle": "/path/to/certificate.p12",  // Primary
  "apiToken": "backup-token"                  // Fallback
}

docs.title: Token Authentication Issues
docs.text: Error Cause Solution 401 Unauthorized Invalid or expired token Regenerate token in F5 XC Console Token not recognized Wrong format Ensure token starts with correct prefix

docs.title: Certificate Authentication Issues
docs.text: Error Cause Solution Certificate not found Wrong path Verify file path exists Invalid certificate Corrupt or wrong format Re-download from F5 XC Console TLS handshake failure Certificate/key mismatch Verify cert and key are paired

docs.title: Debug Mode
docs.text:
Enable debug mode to see authentication details:

const client = createHttpClient(cm, { debug: true });

Or set the environment variable:

export DEBUG=true

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text: This guide covers using environment variables to configure authentication for CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Environment variables provide a flexible way to configure credentials without storing them in files. They take priority over profile settings.

docs.title: Available Variables
docs.text: Variable Required Description F5XC_API_URL Yes F5 XC tenant URL F5XC_API_TOKEN Conditional API token (if using token auth) F5XC_P12_BUNDLE Conditional Path to P12 certificate F5XC_CERT Conditional Path to certificate file F5XC_KEY Conditional Path to private key file F5XC_NAMESPACE No Default namespace F5XC_TLS_INSECURE No Disable TLS verification F5XC_CA_BUNDLE No Path to custom CA bundle

docs.title: API Token
docs.text:
export F5XC_API_URL="https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
export F5XC_API_TOKEN="your-api-token"
export F5XC_NAMESPACE="my-namespace"  # Optional

docs.title: P12 Certificate
docs.text:
export F5XC_API_URL="https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
export F5XC_P12_BUNDLE="/path/to/certificate.p12"

docs.title: Certificate + Key
docs.text:
export F5XC_API_URL="https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
export F5XC_CERT="/path/to/cert.pem"
export F5XC_KEY="/path/to/key.pem"

docs.title: GitHub Actions
docs.text:
.github/workflows/deploy.yml
name: Deploy
on: push

jobs:
  deploy:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      F5XC_API_URL: ${{ secrets.F5XC_API_URL }}
      F5XC_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.F5XC_API_TOKEN }}
      F5XC_NAMESPACE: ${{ vars.F5XC_NAMESPACE }}

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci

      - name: Run deployment
        run: npm run deploy

docs.title: GitLab CI
docs.text:
.gitlab-ci.yml
variables:
  F5XC_API_URL: $F5XC_API_URL
  F5XC_API_TOKEN: $F5XC_API_TOKEN

deploy:
  stage: deploy
  script:
    - npm ci
    - npm run deploy

docs.title: Jenkins
Dockerfile
docs.text:
Dockerfile
FROM node:20-alpine

WORKDIR /app
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --only=production
COPY . .

# Credentials passed at runtime, not build time
CMD ["node", "dist/index.js"]

docs.title: Docker Compose
Docker Run
docs.text:
docker run -e F5XC_API_URL="https://tenant.console.ves.volterra.io" \
           -e F5XC_API_TOKEN="your-token" \
           myapp

docs.title: Secret
docs.text:
secret.yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: f5xc-credentials
type: Opaque
stringData:
  F5XC_API_URL: "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
  F5XC_API_TOKEN: "your-api-token"

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text:
deployment.yaml
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: myapp
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
        - name: app
          image: myapp:latest
          envFrom:
            - secretRef:
                name: f5xc-credentials
          env:
            - name: F5XC_NAMESPACE
              value: "production"

docs.title: Priority Order
docs.text:
Environment variables override profile settings:

  1. Environment Variables (highest priority)
  2. Active Profile
  3. Documentation Mode (lowest priority)

This allows you to:

# Use staging credentials even with production profile active
F5XC_API_URL="https://staging.console.ves.volterra.io" \
F5XC_API_TOKEN="staging-token" \
npm run test

docs.title: Custom CA Bundle
docs.text:
For environments with custom certificate authorities:

export F5XC_CA_BUNDLE="/etc/pki/tls/certs/custom-ca.crt"

docs.title: Insecure Mode (Staging Only)
docs.text:
Warning

Only use for staging/development environments!

export F5XC_TLS_INSECURE="true"

docs.title: Validation in Code
docs.text:
Check if credentials come from environment:

import { CredentialManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const cm = new CredentialManager();
await cm.initialize();

// getActiveProfile() returns null when using env vars
if (!cm.getActiveProfile()) {
  console.log('Using credentials from environment variables');
} else {
  console.log(`Using profile: ${cm.getActiveProfile()}`);
}

docs.title: Variable Not Set
docs.text:
if (!process.env.F5XC_API_URL) {
  console.error('F5XC_API_URL is not set');
  process.exit(1);
}

docs.title: Variable Verification Script
docs.text:
const required = ['F5XC_API_URL'];
const authOptions = ['F5XC_API_TOKEN', 'F5XC_P12_BUNDLE', 'F5XC_CERT'];

// Check required
for (const v of required) {
  if (!process.env[v]) {
    console.error(`Missing required: ${v}`);
    process.exit(1);
  }
}

// Check at least one auth method
const hasAuth = authOptions.some(v => process.env[v]);
if (!hasAuth) {
  console.error('No authentication configured');
  console.error(`Set one of: ${authOptions.join(', ')}`);
  process.exit(1);
}

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text: Issue Solution Variables not loaded Ensure shell profile exports them Variables empty in Docker Check docker-compose or K8s config CI variables not visible Check secret/variable scope Token expired Regenerate in F5 XC Console

docs.title: Profile Management
docs.text: This guide covers managing multiple F5 XC profiles for different tenants and environments.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Profiles allow you to store and switch between different F5 XC configurations:

  • Multiple tenants
  • Different environments (dev, staging, production)
  • Different authentication methods
  • Different default namespaces

docs.title: Using Code
docs.text:
import { getProfileManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

const pm = getProfileManager();

// Create a production profile
await pm.save({
  name: 'production',
  apiUrl: 'https://prod.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: 'prod-token',
  defaultNamespace: 'production'
});

// Create a staging profile
await pm.save({
  name: 'staging',
  apiUrl: 'https://staging.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: 'staging-token',
  defaultNamespace: 'staging',
  tlsInsecure: true  // For staging environments
});

docs.title: Manual Creation
docs.text:
Create a JSON file in ~/.config/f5xc/profiles/:

~/.config/f5xc/profiles/production.json
{
  "name": "production",
  "apiUrl": "https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io",
  "apiToken": "your-api-token",
  "defaultNamespace": "production"
}

Or use YAML format:

~/.config/f5xc/profiles/staging.yaml
name: staging
api_url: https://staging.console.ves.volterra.io
api_token: staging-token
default_namespace: staging
tls_insecure: true

YAML Naming

YAML files use snake_case keys which are automatically converted to camelCase.

docs.title: Listing Profiles
docs.text:
const profiles = await pm.list();

profiles.forEach(profile => {
  const masked = pm.maskProfile(profile);
  console.log(`${profile.name}: ${profile.apiUrl}`);
  console.log(`  Token: ${masked.apiToken}`);
});

Output: 

production: https://prod.console.ves.volterra.io
  Token: ****abcd
staging: https://staging.console.ves.volterra.io
  Token: ****efgh

docs.title: Set Active Profile
docs.text:
const result = await pm.setActive('production');
if (result.success) {
  console.log('Switched to production');
}

docs.title: Get Current Profile
docs.text:
// Get profile name
const name = await pm.getActive();
console.log(`Active profile: ${name}`);

// Get full profile data
const profile = await pm.getActiveProfile();
if (profile) {
  console.log(`Connected to: ${profile.apiUrl}`);
}

docs.title: Updating Profiles
docs.text:
To update a profile, save it with the same name:

const profile = await pm.get('production');
if (profile) {
  // Update the namespace
  profile.defaultNamespace = 'new-namespace';
  await pm.save(profile);
}

docs.title: Deleting Profiles
docs.text:
const result = await pm.delete('old-profile');
if (result.success) {
  console.log('Profile deleted');
} else {
  console.error(result.message);
}

Active Profile

You cannot delete the currently active profile. Switch to another profile first.

docs.title: Profile Validation
docs.text: Profiles are validated when saved:

docs.title: Name Rules
docs.text:
• Alphanumeric characters
  • Dashes (-) and underscores (_)
  • Maximum 64 characters
// Valid names
'production'
'my-tenant-1'
'staging_env'

// Invalid names
'my profile'      // Spaces not allowed
'profile@1'       // Special characters not allowed

docs.title: URL Validation
docs.text:
• Must be a valid HTTP or HTTPS URL
  • URLs are automatically normalized
// These all normalize to the same URL:
'mytenant'
'mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io'
'https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io'
'https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io/'

// Result: https://mytenant.console.ves.volterra.io/api

docs.title: Authentication Required
docs.text:
At least one authentication method must be provided:

// Valid - has apiToken
{ name: 'p1', apiUrl: '...', apiToken: '...' }

// Valid - has p12Bundle
{ name: 'p2', apiUrl: '...', p12Bundle: '/path/to/cert.p12' }

// Valid - has cert and key
{ name: 'p3', apiUrl: '...', cert: '/path/to/cert.pem', key: '/path/to/key.pem' }

// Invalid - no authentication
{ name: 'p4', apiUrl: '...' }  // Error: Profile must have authentication

docs.title: File Permissions
docs.text:
Profile files are created with secure permissions:

  • Profile files: 0600 (owner read/write only)
  • Config directory: 0700 (owner access only)

docs.title: Token Masking
docs.text:
When displaying profiles, tokens are automatically masked:

const masked = pm.maskProfile(profile);
console.log(masked.apiToken);  // ****abcd

docs.title: Profile per Environment
docs.text:
// Development
await pm.save({
  name: 'dev',
  apiUrl: 'https://dev-tenant.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: process.env.DEV_TOKEN!,
  defaultNamespace: 'development'
});

// Staging
await pm.save({
  name: 'staging',
  apiUrl: 'https://staging-tenant.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: process.env.STAGING_TOKEN!,
  defaultNamespace: 'staging',
  tlsInsecure: true
});

// Production
await pm.save({
  name: 'prod',
  apiUrl: 'https://prod-tenant.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: process.env.PROD_TOKEN!,
  defaultNamespace: 'production'
});

docs.title: Profile per Tenant
docs.text:
// Tenant A
await pm.save({
  name: 'tenant-a',
  apiUrl: 'https://tenant-a.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: 'token-a',
  defaultNamespace: 'default'
});

// Tenant B
await pm.save({
  name: 'tenant-b',
  apiUrl: 'https://tenant-b.console.ves.volterra.io',
  apiToken: 'token-b',
  defaultNamespace: 'default'
});

docs.title: Profile Selection Script
docs.text:
import { getProfileManager, CredentialManager, createHttpClient } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function useProfile(name: string) {
  const pm = getProfileManager();

  // Check profile exists
  if (!await pm.exists(name)) {
    throw new Error(`Profile '${name}' not found`);
  }

  // Set active
  await pm.setActive(name);

  // Initialize new credentials
  const cm = new CredentialManager();
  await cm.initialize();

  // Create client
  return createHttpClient(cm);
}

// Usage
const client = await useProfile('production');
const response = await client.get('/web/namespaces');

docs.title: Security Best Practices
docs.text: This guide covers security considerations when using f5xc-auth.

docs.title: Profile Files
docs.text:
Profile files are stored with secure permissions:

  • Profile files: 0600 (owner read/write only)
  • Config directory: 0700 (owner access only)
ls -la ~/.config/f5xc/profiles/
# -rw------- 1 user user 245 Jan 1 12:00 production.json

docs.title: Never Commit Credentials
docs.text:
Add to your .gitignore:

# F5XC credentials
.config/f5xc/
*.p12
*.pem

# Environment files
.env
.env.local
.env.*.local

docs.title: Use Environment Variables in CI/CD
docs.text:
Never hardcode credentials in scripts or configs:

# Good - use secrets
env:
  F5XC_API_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.F5XC_API_TOKEN }}

# Bad - hardcoded
env:
  F5XC_API_TOKEN: "actual-token-value"  # Never do this!

docs.title: Token Best Practices
docs.text:
1. Set expiration dates - Don't use permanent tokens
  2. Rotate regularly - Regenerate tokens periodically
  3. Use minimal scope - Request only needed permissions
  4. One token per environment - Don't share tokens across environments

docs.title: Token Rotation
docs.text:
import { getProfileManager } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

async function rotateToken(profileName: string, newToken: string) {
  const pm = getProfileManager();
  const profile = await pm.get(profileName);

  if (profile) {
    profile.apiToken = newToken;
    await pm.save(profile);
    console.log(`Token rotated for profile: ${profileName}`);
  }
}

docs.title: Token Masking
docs.text:
The library automatically masks tokens when displaying:

const masked = pm.maskProfile(profile);
console.log(masked.apiToken);  // ****abcd (last 4 chars only)

docs.title: Custom CA Bundles
docs.text:
For enterprise environments with internal CAs:

{
  "name": "enterprise",
  "apiUrl": "https://internal.tenant.com",
  "apiToken": "...",
  "caBundle": "/etc/pki/tls/certs/enterprise-ca.crt"
}

Or via environment:

export F5XC_CA_BUNDLE="/etc/pki/tls/certs/enterprise-ca.crt"

docs.title: Insecure Mode Warnings
docs.text:
Never use in production

TLS insecure mode disables certificate verification, making connections vulnerable to MITM attacks.

When insecure mode is enabled, the library outputs a warning:

⚠️  WARNING: TLS certificate verification is DISABLED
   URL: https://staging.tenant.com
   This should ONLY be used for staging/development environments!
   Consider using F5XC_CA_BUNDLE for a more secure solution.

docs.title: When to Use Insecure Mode
docs.text:
Only in these specific scenarios:

  • Local development with self-signed certificates
  • Staging environments with temporary certificates
  • Testing and CI pipelines with mock servers

docs.title: Better Alternatives
docs.text:
Instead of insecure mode, consider:

  1. Custom CA bundle - Add your CA to F5XC_CA_BUNDLE
  2. System trust store - Add CA to system certificates
  3. Proper certificates - Request valid certificates for staging

docs.title: P12 Certificate Handling
docs.text:
// P12 files are read into memory, not stored in profiles
// The path is stored, file is read at runtime
{
  "p12Bundle": "/secure/path/certificate.p12"
}

docs.title: Certificate File Permissions
docs.text:
Ensure certificate files have restricted permissions:

chmod 600 /path/to/certificate.p12
chmod 600 /path/to/cert.pem
chmod 600 /path/to/key.pem

docs.title: Certificate Storage Locations
docs.text: Environment Recommended Location Linux /etc/ssl/private/ or ~/.ssl/ macOS ~/Library/Security/ or ~/.ssl/ Container /run/secrets/ (Docker secrets)

docs.title: Don't Expose Credentials in Errors
docs.text:
try {
  await client.get('/api/endpoint');
} catch (error) {
  // Good - don't log credentials
  console.error('API request failed:', error.message);

  // Bad - might expose sensitive data
  console.error('Full error:', error);  // Could contain auth headers
}

docs.title: Secure Logging
docs.text:
import { logger } from '@robinmordasiewicz/f5xc-auth';

// The library's logger already masks sensitive data
logger.info('Credentials loaded', {
  mode: cm.getAuthMode(),
  tenant: cm.getTenant(),
  // Token is NOT logged
});

docs.title: Before Deployment
docs.text:
• [ ] No hardcoded credentials in code
  • [ ] No credentials in version control
  • [ ] TLS insecure mode disabled
  • [ ] Token expiration set appropriately
  • [ ] Certificate permissions restricted
  • [ ] Environment variables use secrets management
  • [ ] Custom CA bundle configured (if needed)

docs.title: Runtime Security
docs.text:
• [ ] Credentials loaded from secure source
  • [ ] Errors don't expose sensitive data
  • [ ] Logging doesn't include credentials
  • [ ] Token rotation process documented
  • [ ] Incident response plan for credential leak

docs.title: HashiCorp Vault
docs.text:
import Vault from 'node-vault';

const vault = Vault({ endpoint: 'https://vault.example.com' });

async function loadFromVault() {
  const secret = await vault.read('secret/data/f5xc');

  process.env.F5XC_API_URL = secret.data.data.api_url;
  process.env.F5XC_API_TOKEN = secret.data.data.api_token;

  // Now initialize f5xc-auth
  const cm = new CredentialManager();
  await cm.initialize();
  return cm;
}

docs.title: AWS Secrets Manager
docs.text:
import { SecretsManagerClient, GetSecretValueCommand } from '@aws-sdk/client-secrets-manager';

const client = new SecretsManagerClient({ region: 'us-east-1' });

async function loadFromAWS() {
  const response = await client.send(
    new GetSecretValueCommand({ SecretId: 'f5xc/credentials' })
  );

  const secret = JSON.parse(response.SecretString!);

  process.env.F5XC_API_URL = secret.api_url;
  process.env.F5XC_API_TOKEN = secret.api_token;

  const cm = new CredentialManager();
  await cm.initialize();
  return cm;
}

docs.title: Kubernetes Secrets
docs.text:
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: f5xc-credentials
  annotations:
    # For external secrets operator
    external-secrets.io/refresh-interval: "1h"
type: Opaque
stringData:
  F5XC_API_URL: "https://tenant.console.ves.volterra.io"
  F5XC_API_TOKEN: "your-api-token"

docs.title: If Credentials Are Leaked
docs.text:
1. Immediately rotate - Generate new token in F5 XC Console
  2. Update all references - Update profiles and environment variables
  3. Audit access - Review F5 XC audit logs for unauthorized access
  4. Identify source - Determine how leak occurred
  5. Prevent recurrence - Implement controls to prevent future leaks

docs.title: Audit Logging
docs.text:
Monitor F5 XC audit logs for:

  • Unusual API access patterns
  • Access from unexpected IP addresses
  • Failed authentication attempts
  • High-frequency API calls
